description: Fix CLI UX friction points found during fresh-user experience testing. Issues range from misleading MCP config output when port is changed but server not restarted, to confusing default behavior when running opentabs with no args, to cosmetic issues in plugin search output. LOW severity items documented here but not given stories: (1) first-time setup prints secret in plaintext to terminal (intentional for discoverability), (2) npx create-opentabs-plugin returns 404 (private package limitation, no workaround).

userStories.title: Warn when config show --show-secret displays MCP URLs with a pending port change
userStories.description: As a user who changed the port via config set but hasn't restarted the server, I want config show --show-secret to warn me that the displayed MCP URLs use the config port which may differ from the running server, so that I don't copy non-functional configs.
acceptanceCriteria: When the server is running and config port differs from the running server's port, config show --show-secret prints a yellow warning below the MCP client configs like: 'Note: The server is currently running on port <running_port>. Restart the server for port <config_port> to take effect.'
When the server is not running, no warning is printed (port mismatch is moot)
When config port matches the running server's port, no warning is printed
The detection works by fetching /health on the config port and falling back to the default port (9515) to check if the server is running on either
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, handleConfigShow() around line 38-187. The function already reads the config port at line 59 and has access to secret. Add a helper that probes the running server's /health endpoint to get its actual port (or detect it's not running). If the server responds on a different port than what config says, print a warning after the MCP client configs block. The health endpoint response includes the port indirectly (you know which port you queried). A simple approach: try fetching /health on the config port — if it fails with connection refused, try the default port 9515 — if either succeeds but on a different port than config.port, warn. Only do this probe when --show-secret is active (when MCP configs are displayed).

userStories.title: Skip no-op port change in config set port
userStories.description: As a user, when I run opentabs config set port 9515 and the port is already 9515, I want the CLI to tell me the value is unchanged rather than saying 'Restart the MCP server for the port change to take effect', so that I'm not confused about whether a restart is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: When config set port <value> is called and config.port already equals <value>, print 'port: <value> (unchanged)' and do NOT print the restart warning
When config set port <value> changes the port to a new value, the existing behavior (print new value + restart warning + notify server) is preserved
When config has no port field (new config) and user sets the default port 9515, it still writes to config but prints '(unchanged)' since the effective port was already 9515
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, handleSetPort() around line 350-365. Before writing the config, compare newPort to the current config.port (or DEFAULT_PORT if not set). If they're equal, print the unchanged message and return early without writing config or notifying the server. Import DEFAULT_PORT from @opentabs-dev/shared (already imported at line 16).

userStories.title: Show help text when opentabs is run with no arguments and server is not running
userStories.description: As a new user who just installed the CLI, when I run opentabs with no arguments and the server is not running, I want to see help information about available commands in addition to the 'not running' message, so that I know what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: When opentabs is run with no args and the server is not running, the output includes 'Server not running' AND a brief list of useful commands (at minimum: 'opentabs start', 'opentabs --help')
When opentabs is run with no args and the server IS running, behavior is unchanged (shows status)
Exit code remains 1 when server is not running
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/cli.ts, the default action around line 36-43. Currently it delegates to handleStatus() which prints 'Server not running / Start it with: opentabs start' and exits 1. The fix should be in cli.ts: after the handleStatus() call catches the 'not running' case, or alternatively modify the catch block in handleStatus (platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts around line 177-195) to also print a hint like 'Run opentabs --help to see all available commands.' after the 'Start it with' line. The simplest approach is to add one line to the isConnectionRefused branch in status.ts: console.error(pc.dim('Run opentabs --help to see all available commands.'));

userStories.title: Handle empty plugin description gracefully in search output
userStories.description: As a user searching for plugins, when a plugin has no npm description, I want the search output to omit the empty dash separator or show a placeholder, so the output doesn't look broken with a double space.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin has no description, the search output line does NOT show an empty '— ' before 'by author' — either omit the dash entirely or show '(no description)'
When a plugin has a description, the output is unchanged
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts, handlePluginSearch() around line 415-424. The line that prints each result is: console.log(`  ${label} ${pc.bold(pkg.name)} ${pc.dim(`v${pkg.version}`)} — ${desc} ${pc.dim(`by ${author}`)}`); When desc is empty string, this produces '—  by author' (double space). Fix: conditionally include the '— ${desc}' part only when desc is non-empty. For example: const descPart = desc ? `— ${desc} ` : ''; and then use descPart in the template.

userStories.title: Remove redundant enabled entries from browserToolPolicy on re-enable
userStories.description: As a user, when I disable a browser tool and then re-enable it, I want the enabled entry to be removed from config since enabled is the default state, so that config show output stays clean.
acceptanceCriteria: When config set browser-tool.<name> enabled is called and the tool is currently disabled, the entry is removed from browserToolPolicy in config.json rather than set to true
When config set browser-tool.<name> disabled is called, behavior is unchanged (sets to false)
config show no longer shows 'browser_execute_script: enabled' after re-enabling a previously disabled tool
The server is still notified after the change
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, handleSetBrowserTool() around line 313-348. When value is 'enabled', instead of setting policy[toolName] = true, delete the key: delete policy[toolName]. This way the config only contains entries for disabled tools. The server-side browserToolPolicy logic treats missing keys as enabled (the default), so removing the key has the same runtime effect as setting it to true.
